target: ISS-2329 / HOLISTIC-HOL-REDUNDANCY.c2 hole 6 (moderate) — adopt the c1 stronger_arguments reading-guide recommendation. Add a 3-sentence paragraph under the existing 'How This Review Is Organized' h2 in part1 (Overview) that (a) names the analytical lenses each Part applies, (b) names the five recurring arguments (eclipse non-discrimination, aetheric-refraction unfalsifiability, Sydney–Perth coordinate-loop, relabeling-as-prediction, OTS wrong-side fix) and points to their canonical hubs with anchor links, and (c) inoculates against the 'repetitive' defender charge by clarifying the redundancy is across lenses rather than within any one of them.
replacement_html: Cross-tab structure. Within the three tiers above, the review applies several analytical lenses — geometry (Part 2), automation code (Part 2b), per-claim evaluation (Part 3), live-prediction dashboards (Part 4), kill-shot binary tests (Part 5), predictions-page structure (Part 6), falsification tests (Part 7), AI-context directives (Part 8), and surviving conclusions (Part 9) — so a small number of structural problems with the dome surface from many directions rather than one. A handful of arguments — eclipse non-discrimination (canonical hub: §4.2), aetheric-refraction unfalsifiability (§1.5), the Sydney–Perth coordinate-loop disagreement (§2.9), the relabeling-of-standard-physics-as-dome-predictions pattern (§2b.2), and the OpenTimestamps wrong-side methodology error (Timestamp Error tab) — recur across tabs because each lens raises the same problem with different evidence; the cross-references above mark the canonical statement, with other appearances now serving as one-line pointers. A reader (or AI auditor) who finds the review repetitive should check that the repetition holds across these lenses, not within any one of them.
kernel_of_truth: The dome defender (and an AI auditor) reading the review linearly does encounter the same arguments repeatedly — eclipse non-discrimination appears in part4 (144 mentions), part5 (20), part6 (85), part8 (13), part8b (39); aetheric refraction in §1.5 and §7.11 both re-derive the n(r) formula; Sydney–Perth appears in four places; the relabeling pattern in §2b.2 and §6.4; the OTS argument in part4, part6, part8, part8b. The curmudgeon c2 finding (HOL-REDUNDANCY) confirms this directly: 'the document is structurally LESS consolidated than at c1.' The defender's 'repetitive' charge is not without merit — the structural redundancy is real, even when each instance serves a different analytical purpose. The c1 stronger_arguments recommendation (a reading guide explaining the intentional structure) is the kernel-of-truth response: acknowledge that the same problems are restated across tabs, explain why (multiple lenses, one model), and point to the canonical hub for each recurring argument so a tab-skipping reader can find the load-bearing statement directly. The reading guide does not fix the underlying consolidation work that holes 0–4 of HOL-REDUNDANCY.c2 still recommend; it does, however, provide the cross-reference scaffolding those consolidations will need, and it pre-empts the defender charge without requiring the consolidations to land first.
why_it_doesnt_save_claim_for_dome_defend: The reading guide changes the framing of the redundancy from 'the reviewer is padding their case' to 'the reviewer evaluates the same problem from multiple analytical lenses.' That framing is honest — Part 2 (geometry) and Part 2b (code) genuinely are different analytical lenses on the dome model, not duplicate critiques. But the reading guide alone does not consolidate the eclipse cluster (hole 0), aetheric refraction (hole 1), OTS (hole 2), ensemble-anchor (hole 3), or the relabeling cluster (hole 6). It explains the layering and names the hubs; it does not yet trim the spoke prose down to one-sentence references. The consolidations remain necessary work in their own EXPs. The narrow argumentative payoff of this EXP is: (1) the defender charge 'this review repeats itself' now has a one-sentence preemptive response visible at the top of the document; (2) the canonical-hub designations are stated explicitly so subsequent consolidation EXPs (and a tab-skipping reader) have an authoritative anchor to point at; (3) the OTS hub is correctly named as the Timestamp Error tab — which is the actual canonical home declared in build-scripts/generate-html.js ('This tab is the canonical home for our OpenTimestamps argument'), correcting the curmudgeon c2 hole 2 misdiagnosis that no dedicated section exists (it does exist, just not in sections.json — it is wired into the generator).

patches.find_text: The remaining parts confirm and extend that conclusion with independent evidence from progressively broader domains.
patches.replace_text:
The remaining parts confirm and extend that conclusion with independent evidence from progressively broader domains.

Cross-tab structure. Within the three tiers above, the review applies several analytical lenses — geometry (Part 2), automation code (Part 2b), per-claim evaluation (Part 3), live-prediction dashboards (Part 4), kill-shot binary tests (Part 5), predictions-page structure (Part 6), falsification tests (Part 7), AI-context directives (Part 8), and surviving conclusions (Part 9) — so a small number of structural problems with the dome surface from many directions rather than one. A handful of arguments — eclipse non-discrimination (canonical hub: §4.2), aetheric-refraction unfalsifiability (§1.5), the Sydney–Perth coordinate-loop disagreement (§2.9), the relabeling-of-standard-physics-as-dome-predictions pattern (§2b.2), and the OpenTimestamps wrong-side methodology error (Timestamp Error tab) — recur across tabs because each lens raises the same problem with different evidence; the cross-references above mark the canonical statement, with other appearances now serving as one-line pointers. A reader (or AI auditor) who finds the review repetitive should check that the repetition holds across these lenses, not within any one of them.
patches.rationale: Inserts the cross-tab-structure reading-guide paragraph into the existing <div class="evidence-roadmap"> in part1, immediately after the 'This ordering is deliberate...' paragraph that closes the tier explanation and before the 'Living review' paragraph. The new paragraph (a) names the analytical lenses Parts 2–9 apply, (b) enumerates the five recurring arguments and their canonical-hub anchor links, and (c) inoculates against the 'repetitive' defender charge by clarifying the redundancy holds across lenses rather than within any one of them. Anchor IDs verified against current sections.json: section-1-5 (Aetheric Refraction mechanism), 2-9-the-v13-coordinate-system-a-self-referential-loop (Sydney–Perth canonical), 2b-2-relabeling-standard-physics, eclipse-analysis (canonical eclipse hub anchor in part4), and timestamp-error (the canonical OTS hub, declared in build-scripts/generate-html.js — note this corrects the c2 hole 2 misdiagnosis that no dedicated section exists). find_text verified to occur exactly once in data/sections.json. No existing prose is removed; this is a pure addition.

holes_addressed: HOLISTIC-HOL-REDUNDANCY.c2 hole 6 (moderate) — c1 stronger_arguments reading-guide recommendation adopted. 3-sentence cross-tab-structure paragraph added under the existing 'How This Review Is Organized' h2 in part1 Overview. Names the analytical lenses Parts 2–9 apply, the five recurring arguments and their canonical hubs (with anchor links), and the across-lenses-not-within-each framing that answers the defender 'repetitive' charge.

anticipated_objections.objection: The new paragraph names §8.5 indirectly (via the Timestamp Error tab, which §8.5 is a spoke of). c2 hole 2 said the OTS canonical hub does not exist — should we wait for hole 2 work to land first?
anticipated_objections.response: No. The Timestamp Error tab IS the canonical OTS home — it is declared as such in build-scripts/generate-html.js line 1903 ('This tab is the canonical home for our OpenTimestamps argument. Three other sections touch the same critique from different analytical lenses'). The curmudgeon c2 hole 2 missed this because the tab is wired directly into the generator rather than living in sections.json. The reading guide names the actual canonical home; hole 2 work (consolidating the §4.6 / §6.10 / §6.11 / §8.5 / §8b.3 spokes down to one-line references) remains valid and proceeds independently. Naming the canonical home now, before the spoke consolidations land, is exactly what holes 5 and 6 recommend doing first — the cross-reference scaffolding is a prerequisite for the consolidation work, not a downstream artifact.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why three sentences and not two as the curmudgeon recommendation allowed?
anticipated_objections.response: The recommendation said '2–3 sentences' and explicitly required the third sentence to 'do double duty: explains the redundancy AND creates the cross-reference scaffolding the consolidations above need.' Compressing to two sentences either drops the lens enumeration (sentence 1) or drops the canonical-hub anchor links (sentence 2) — both of which are explicitly required. The third sentence is the across-vs-within-lenses inoculation that closes the defender charge. Three sentences is the minimum that does all three jobs the curmudgeon listed.

anticipated_objections.objection: The new paragraph adds about 178 words. That is more than a typical 2–3-sentence paragraph because of the parenthetical lens list and the five anchor-linked hub references. Does the length defeat the 'brief reading guide' intent?
anticipated_objections.response: The word count is inflated by the parentheticals and inline anchor links, not by additional argumentation. The paragraph still scans as three readable sentences — sentence 1 names the lenses (one parenthetical), sentence 2 names the recurring arguments with their hub anchors (one parenthetical per argument), sentence 3 closes with the inoculation. A skim-reader who skips the parentheticals reads three short sentences. A reader who follows the anchor links gets the cross-reference scaffolding the curmudgeon explicitly required. The alternative — listing hubs in a separate bullet list below the paragraph — would add structural weight to the Overview that the curmudgeon did not request.

anticipated_objections.objection: The paragraph adopts a 'defensive' framing (pre-empting a charge) at the top of the Overview. Does that hurt the document tone?
anticipated_objections.response: The framing is structural-explanatory, not defensive. The paragraph explains the document architecture (lenses applied per Part), names what recurs and where the canonical statement lives, and tells the reader how to navigate. The inoculation is the third sentence and is read by both honest skim-readers and defenders — it is the same advice for both audiences ('check that the repetition holds across lenses'). The Overview already contains a 'Symmetric standards' paragraph that explicitly addresses the criteria-symmetry charge; adding a parallel structural-explanation paragraph maintains that meta-discussion register without adding new defensive tone. The c1 stronger_arguments recommendation explicitly framed this as 'pre-empt the repetitive criticism,' and curmudgeon c2 hole 6 confirmed the recommendation by re-recommending it — the project treats this as architecture documentation, not apology.

anticipated_objections.objection: The newly-named canonical hubs (§4.2 for eclipse, §1.5 for aetheric refraction, §2.9 for Sydney–Perth, §2b.2 for relabeling, Timestamp Error tab for OTS) effectively commit the project to those designations. Is that authoritative declaration appropriate without the corresponding spoke consolidations?
anticipated_objections.response: Yes, with the caveat noted in summary_for_decider: the curmudgeon c2 review explicitly recommends each of these hubs (§4.2 hole 0, §1.5 hole 1, §2.9 hole 4, §2b.2 hole 6, and 'either §8.5 OR a new dedicated section' hole 2 — superseded by the actual canonical home being the Timestamp Error tab per build-scripts/generate-html.js). EXP-495 already consolidated Sydney–Perth content per these hub designations. The reading guide states what is already the project's working position; subsequent consolidation EXPs trim the spokes. Future EXPs that re-evaluate a hub designation can either (a) edit the reading guide to reflect a new hub, or (b) keep the current hub and refute the would-be replacement — but having a stated hub is strictly more useful than having no stated hub.

anticipated_objections.objection: Should the new paragraph also name the QUADRUPLE-FAILURE headline stat (hole 4 of HOLISTIC-HOL-REDUNDANCY.c2 is a separate issue, but the headline is missing site-wide per ISS-2337)?
anticipated_objections.response: No — that is the scope of EXP-501 / ISS-2337, a separate item. Conflating the two would (a) widen this EXP beyond the c1 stronger_arguments reading-guide recommendation it is explicitly answering, (b) make the new paragraph longer than the 2–3-sentence brief, and (c) risk the QUADRUPLE-FAILURE framing landing in the wrong place (the curmudgeon recommendation for that issue is a top-of-review headline stat, not a sub-clause of the cross-tab-structure explanation). Keeping the scope narrow to the c1 reading-guide recommendation lets both EXPs land cleanly and be reviewed independently.

decider_action_recommended: Apply patch P1 to data/sections.json (part1 Overview, single find-replace, one occurrence — verified). Run tests/run.sh and the full build to confirm (a) the new paragraph renders inside the existing <div class="evidence-roadmap"> block of part1 between the 'This ordering is deliberate' paragraph and the 'Living review' paragraph; (b) all five inline anchor links resolve to the named targets (section-1-5, 2-9-the-v13-coordinate-system-a-self-referential-loop, 2b-2-relabeling-standard-physics, eclipse-analysis, timestamp-error) and (c) no test in tests/ regresses. Close ISS-2329 with resolution 'reading-guide paragraph added to part1 Overview per c1 stronger_arguments / c2 HOL-REDUNDANCY hole 6 recommendation; names analytical lenses Parts 2–9 apply, the five recurring arguments and their canonical-hub anchors, and the across-vs-within-lenses inoculation; cross-reference scaffolding for hole 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 6 consolidation EXPs is now in place.'
summary_for_decider: ISS-2329 (moderate, HOL-REDUNDANCY c2 hole 6) — c1 stronger_arguments reading-guide recommendation. Adds a 3-sentence cross-tab-structure paragraph under the existing 'How This Review Is Organized' h2 in part1 Overview. Sentence 1: names the analytical lenses each Part applies (geometry / code / per-claim / dashboards / kill-shots / predictions-page / falsification / AI-directives / conclusions). Sentence 2: names the five recurring arguments (eclipse non-discrimination, aetheric refraction, Sydney–Perth, relabeling, OTS) and links to their canonical hubs by anchor (#eclipse-analysis, #section-1-5, #2-9-the-v13-coordinate-system-a-self-referential-loop, #2b-2-relabeling-standard-physics, #timestamp-error). Sentence 3: inoculates the 'repetitive' defender charge by clarifying the redundancy is across lenses rather than within any one of them. Patch is a single additive find-replace with the find_text closing the 'This ordering is deliberate' paragraph and the replace_text appending the new paragraph; verified to occur exactly once in data/sections.json. Notable: the OTS hub is correctly named as the Timestamp Error tab (declared canonical in build-scripts/generate-html.js line 1903), which corrects the curmudgeon c2 hole 2 misdiagnosis that no dedicated OTS section exists (the tab is generator-wired, not sections.json-wired — c2 hole 2 looked only in sections.json). No spoke consolidation work is done in this EXP — that remains hole 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 6 scope in separate EXPs. The reading guide provides the cross-reference scaffolding those consolidations will need. review_class: deep-attack (per tracker; the cross-reference framing is new argumentative content, not refinement of existing prose).
